steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses an em dash after the appositive phrase the cellular organs that generate energy from light. When an appositive is set off by an em dash on the closing side, a matching em dash is required on the opening side — immediately after the noun being renamed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — chloroplasts;: a semicolon separates independent clauses, not an appositive from its noun. ✗
  • Choice B — chloroplasts: no punctuation fails to mark the boundary of the appositive at all. ✗
  • Choice C — chloroplasts,: a comma mismatches with the closing em dash; both sides must use the same punctuation mark. ✗
  • Choice D — chloroplasts—: the opening em dash matches the closing em dash, correctly framing the appositive. ✓
D is correct.
